units.title: Present Simple Foundation
units.focus: Use Present Simple for habits, routines, facts, and schedules.
objectives: Recognize when Present Simple is the right tense.
Build affirmative, negative, and question forms.

sections.title: Meaning
body: Present Simple describes habits, routines, general truths, permanent situations, and schedules.
It usually answers the question: what do you usually do?

structures.pattern: Subject + base verb (+ s/es for he/she/it)
structures.example: He works as an IT engineer.
He does not work on Sundays.

mistakes.why: He/she/it needs -s in affirmative Present Simple.

mistakes.wrong: He does not works on Sundays.
mistakes.right: He does not work on Sundays.
mistakes.why: Does already carries the third-person marker, so the main verb returns to base form.

examples.sentence: Dario checks his email before he starts coding.
examples.note: This describes a regular work routine.

examples.sentence: He drops off his kids at school every morning.
examples.note: Every morning signals a repeated habit.

exercises.explanation: Dario is he, so the verb work becomes works.

exercises.prompt: Change to a question: He works in IT.
exercises.explanation: Use does + subject + base verb for he/she/it questions.

exercises.prompt: Choose the tense: Dario checks his email every morning.
exercises.explanation: Every morning signals a repeated routine, so Simple Present is the best tense.
